basic_info.description_header: Why do I want to be a Blockchain developer?
basic_info.description: My motivation to become a Blockchain developer stems from a deep-seated curiosity and a relentless drive for exploration. I see exploration as a pursuit of unchanging truth, accessible to all. Blockchain technology, in its essence, serves as a transparent and incorruptible ledger that records truth. It is this integrity and transparency that attract me to blockchain. I am eager to harness this technology to develop services that contribute meaningfully to society, ensuring that every transaction and interaction is anchored in trust and authenticity.

projects.title: Web3 Project: Naegift
projects.description: Naegift is a decentralized gift exchange marketplace where users can register or purchase gifts to send. Transactions are initiated using MetaMask, and escrow services facilitated through smart contracts on the Ethereum blockchain mediate the transactions. The core technology involves the use of distributed identities and verifiable credentials (DID/VC) to securely manage identities and ensure gift ownership, powered by Masca and Veramo.

projects.title: React Project: Stacker-Labs
projects.description: Stacker-Labs is a platform where developers can create and share code snippets. Users can create an account, share and search for code snippets categorized by tags, and follow other users for communication. Global states such as dark mode and user information are managed through Recoil, and Socket.io is used for real-time chatting with followed users.

projects.title: Express Project: DevUp
projects.description: DevUp is a platform designed for developers to communicate and collaborate with each other, which I designed and implemented the layout using Figma. It features local and social login capabilities, and logged-in users can create profiles, write posts, and perform searches. The home screen supports infinite scrolling, and users can participate in public chats implemented with Socket.io. It is rendered on the server-side with ExpressJS using the Nunjucks template engine.

projects.title: Kakao Login Process
projects.description: This project is a simple implementation of the Kakao login process using OAuth2.0. The project demonstrates how to authenticate users using Kakao's OAuth2.0 API and retrieve user information. The project is built using Express.js, based on the sequence diagram.

projects.title: Express CRUD Project
projects.description: This is a simple CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) bulletin board for a bootcamp academy. It is divided into a notice board accessible only by administrators and a suggestion board accessible to logged-in general users. CRUD operations are performed according to RESTful API on an ExpressJS server, interacting with the DB through MySQL and a hardcoded ORM.

experience.title: Kyungil Game Academy
technologies: CRUD Ops, REST APIs, HTTP Protocols
